div1.entryFree:
Nursery, 1) the apartment where young children are nursed and brought up: from their n. were stolen, Cymb. I, 1, 59.
2) tender care and attendance: I loved her most and thought to set my rest on her kind n. Lr. I, 1, 126.
3) that which brings up or engenders something: fair Padua, n. of arts, Shr. I, 1, 2. it well may serve a n. to our gentry, All's I, 2, 16 (a school of war). must or now be cropped, or, shedding, breed a n. of like evil, Troil. I, 3, 319.
